# statuses

[![NPM Version][npm-version-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][npm-downloads-image]][npm-url]
[![Node.js Version][node-version-image]][node-version-url]
[![Build Status][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

HTTP status utility for Node.js

This module provides a list of status codes and messages from the following
resources:

  * [IANA Status Code Registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/http-status-codes/http-status-codes.xhtml)
  * [Node.js HTTP library](https://nodejs.org/dist/latest/docs/api/http.html#http_http_status_codes)
  * [NGINX Web Server](https://nginx.org/en/docs/http/ngx_http_request.h)
  * [Apache HTTP Server](https://svn.apache.org/repos/asf/httpd/httpd/trunk/modules/http/http_protocol.c)

## Installation

This is a [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/) module available through the
[npm registry](https://www.npmjs.com/). Installation is done using the
[`npm install` command](https://docs.npmjs.com/getting-started/installing-npm-packages-locally):

```sh
$ npm install statuses
```

## API

<!-- eslint-disable no-unused-vars -->

```js
var status = require('statuses')
```

### status(code)

Returns the status code number from a code or message.

```js
status(403) // => 403
status('403') // => 403
status('forbidden') // => 403
status('Forbidden') // => 403
status(306) // throws, as it's not supported by node.js
```

### status.STATUS_CODES

A map of status codes to status messages, like Node.js's
[http.STATUS_CODES](https://nodejs.org/dist/latest/docs/api/http.html#http_http_status_codes).

### status.codes

Array of all the status codes as integers.

### status.redirect

An object with the status codes that are redirects.

### status.empty

An object with the status codes that have an empty body.

### status.retry

An object with the status codes that should be retried.

### status.isRedirect(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is a valid redirect status.

```js
status.isRedirect(302) // => true
status.isRedirect(200) // => false
```

### status.isEmpty(code)

Returns `true` if a status code expects an empty body.

```js
status.isEmpty(204) // => true
status.isEmpty(200) // => false
```

### status.isRetry(code)

Returns `true` if you should retry the rest.

```js
status.isRetry(503) // => true
status.isRetry(200) // => false
```

### status.isError(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is an error (4xx or 5xx).

```js
status.isError(503) // => true
status.isError(200) // => false
```

### status.isClientError(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is a client error (4xx).

```js
status.isClientError(403) // => true
status.isClientError(500) // => false
```

### status.isServerError(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is a server error (5xx).

```js
status.isServerError(503) // => true
status.isServerError(403) // => false
```

### status.isInformational(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is an informational code (1xx).

```js
status.isInformational(103) // => true
status.isInformational(403) // => false
```

### status.isSuccess(code)

Returns `true` if a status code is a success code (2xx).

```js
status.isSuccess(200) // => true
status.isSuccess(403) // => false
```
